rationale_for_baseline_tier: A superficial abrasion is low-acuity, BUT this one is contaminated with embedded grit and the patient is tetanus-prone, so it needs prompt (same-day/next-day) in-person wound care (irrigation/debridement) and a tetanus booster — things telehealth cannot deliver (T2). A deep/joint injury routes to ED (T4); secondary cellulitis over the following days routes to GP/ED.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: The wound is actually deep/gaping with exposed fat, muscle, tendon or bone, or involves a joint, or there is restricted movement or numbness
escalation_edges.communication_template: If it's actually a deep gaping wound with visible fat/muscle/tendon, involves a joint, or you can't move the knee or feel the foot — go to the Emergency Department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Over the following days: spreading hot red rash from the wound, thick yellow pus, a red streak toward the groin, or fever
escalation_edges.communication_template: If over the next days the wound spreads a hot red rash, oozes thick yellow pus, gets a red streak heading toward your groin, or you get a fever — see a doctor / go to ED that day.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone; no sterile supplies at home
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Arrange in-person wound care since adequate irrigation/debridement and tetanus can't be done at home.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Rinse it with clean water and cover it with a non-stick dressing now, but get it seen promptly (today/tomorrow) so the grit can be properly cleaned out and you can have a tetanus booster. Keep the dressing on and the wound moist.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Prompt in-person GP/practice-nurse wound care + tetanus; ED for deep/joint injury or infection
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: ED/urgent care if you can't get in-person wound care promptly or if it's deep.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Contaminated superficial right-knee abrasion with embedded grit; tetanus-prone (uncertain/>10y) — needs irrigation/debridement + tetanus booster; deep/joint injury screened negative; non-adherent moist dressing; cellulitis safety-net.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Home dressing only, not arranging decontamination/tetanus.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Embedded grit causes infection/tattooing, or tetanus develops in an under-immunised patient.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No tetanus/decontamination plan or no infection safety-net.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/ED for a simple graze that just needs practice-nurse wound care and a booster — unnecessary.
